findings.description: Title and certification text lost their original visual prominence and alignment.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the title is large, centered-left on the page with substantial spacing, and the certification statement is also large and broken into multiple lines. In the HTML, the title is much smaller, left-aligned near the top, and the certification text is reduced to small body text on a single line.

findings.description: Overall cover-page layout and whitespace grouping are not preserved.
findings.evidence: The PDF presents a cover-style page with content positioned lower on the page and large empty space around two distinct text blocks. The HTML compresses both text blocks to the top-left area, adds a horizontal rule, and removes the original vertical spacing and grouping.
